title: git-toolkit configuration v2
description: Configuration file for git-toolkit — manages Git multi-account environments across multiple providers (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Forgejo, Bitbucket).

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for editor validation and autocompletion.

global.description: Global settings: root directory for clones, SSH and GCM credential configuration.

folder.description: Root directory for all git repositories. Supports ~ prefix for home directory or absolute paths.

enabled.description: Enable SSH credential management (key generation, ssh-config setup).

ssh_folder.description: Path to the SSH configuration directory. Supports ~ prefix. Defaults to ~/.ssh.

enabled.description: Enable Git Credential Manager for HTTPS authentication.

helper.description: Credential helper type. Typically 'manager' for GCM.

credential_store.description: OS-specific credential storage backend. wincredman = Windows Credential Manager, keychain = macOS Keychain, secretservice = Linux GNOME Keyring / KWallet.

accounts.description: Map of account identifiers to account configurations. Each account represents WHO you are on a git server — one unique (hostname, username) pair. The map key is a human-friendly ID (e.g., 'github-LuisPalacios') also used as the default first-level clone folder.

additionalProperties.description: A Git provider account — identity and credentials for one server login.

provider.description: Git hosting provider type. Determines which API is used for discovery, migration, and mirrors. Use 'generic' for providers without API integration.

url.description: Provider base URL (scheme + host only, no path). E.g., 'https://github.com' or 'https://git.parchis.org'.

username.description: Account username on the Git provider. Used for authentication and API calls.

name.description: Default git user.name for commits in repos under this account.

email.description: Default git user.email for commits in repos under this account.

default_branch.description: Default branch name (e.g., 'main', 'master'). Used for sync operations.

default_credential_type.description: Default authentication method for repos in this account. Repos inherit this unless they override it. 'gcm' = Git Credential Manager (HTTPS), 'ssh' = SSH key pair, 'token' = Personal Access Token.

ssh.description: SSH authentication configuration for this account.

host.description: SSH config Host alias (e.g., 'gh-LuisPalacios'). Used in clone URLs: git@gh-LuisPalacios:org/repo.git

hostname.description: Real SSH server hostname (e.g., 'github.com'). Written to ~/.ssh/config.

key_type.description: SSH key type to generate. ed25519 is recommended.

gcm.description: Git Credential Manager configuration for this account.

provider.description: GCM provider hint for the OAuth authentication flow.

use_http_path.description: If true, GCM scopes credentials by full HTTP path (one credential per repo). If false, credentials are shared per domain/user.

sources.description: Map of source identifiers to source configurations. Each source defines WHAT you clone from an account. The map key is used as the default first-level clone folder (unless overridden by the 'folder' field).

additionalProperties.description: A clone source — references an account and lists repositories to manage.

account.description: References an account key from the 'accounts' section. Defines which credentials and identity to use.

folder.description: Override the first-level clone folder. If omitted, the source map key is used. Path under global.folder.

repos.description: Map of repository keys to repo configurations. Keys use 'org/repo' format (e.g., 'familia/ines-denia', 'LuisPalacios/git-toolkit'). The org part becomes the second-level folder, the repo part becomes the third-level (clone) folder.

additionalProperties.description: Configuration for a single repository. All fields are optional — repos inherit defaults from the account.

credential_type.description: Override authentication method for this repo. If omitted, inherits from account's default_credential_type.

name.description: Override git user.name for this specific repo.

email.description: Override git user.email for this specific repo.

id_folder.description: Override the second-level directory (org folder). Default: the part before '/' in the repo key. Always relative.

clone_folder.description: Override the third-level directory (clone folder). Default: the part after '/' in the repo key. If absolute (starts with '/', '~', or '../'), replaces the entire clone path, ignoring source folder and id_folder.
